title: Cesi Eirriss
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Otheruses|Eirriss}} {{Character |type=Rebel |image=[[File:Doc.jpg]] |name=Cesi "Doc" Eirriss |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], [[Commenor system/Legends|Commenor system]]''[[Star Wars Omnibus: The Other Sons of Tatooine]]'' places the events of ''[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron ½]]'', which features Cesi Eirriss' [[Death/Legends|death]] in the [[Commenor system/Legends|Commenor system]], concurrent with those of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], which takes place in 0 BBY per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. |species=[[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]][[X-Wing Rogue Squadron ½|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' ½]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]] |pronouns=She/her |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes=[[Color/Legends|Green]][[Empire 12|''Empire'' 12]] |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] **[[Red Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Red Squadron]]''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Join formation, "Doc"—that's an order!''"
"''Remember what Porkins said? Sometimes you have to sacrifice to win the hand…!|Wedge Antilles and Cesi Eirriss|}} '''Cesi "Doc" Eirriss''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] who served in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic's]] [[Red Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Red Squadron]] prior to the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. Around [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] she accompanied [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], [[Biggs Darklighter/Legends|Biggs Darklighter]] and [[Jek Tono Porkins/Legends|Jek Tono Porkins]] to [[Commenor/Legends|Commenor]] to [[Mission to Commenor (0 BBY)|escort]] [[Nera Dantels]] and her badly needed cargo of [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droids]] to [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. Pursued by [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Fighters]] shortly after leaving Commenor, Eirriss's [[Starship/Legends|ship]] was hit by debris from an exploding TIE [[Solar collector/Legends|solar panel]], damaging her [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|deflector shield generator]] and [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]]. Antilles ordered her to abandon ship, but Eirriss knew that a recovery would take away precious time from their escape and directed her [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] into a collision course with the pursuing TIEs. Eirriss was killed when her ship was destroyed in the resulting collision, though the explosion eliminated a number of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] and gave the other Rebels a chance to escape to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. ==Biography== ===Early career=== [[File:EirrissHasbro.jpg|thumb|left|240px|Cesi Eirriss, political scientist and Rebel pilot]] By [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], Cesi Eirriss attained a doctorate in political science. However, the [[Gender/Legends|woman's]] career took a different path when she joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] as a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]]. By the time of the [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|Destruction of Alderaan]], she was based at the [[Great Temple/Legends|Rebel base]] on the [[Yavin 4/Legends|fourth moon]] of the [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]]''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'' [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin Prime]], where she was assigned to [[Red Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Red Squadron]]. Her doctorate earned her the nickname "Doc" among the other pilots. The Alliance was in need of additional starfighters for use in their [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|war]] against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. After they learned of a number of [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]] at an [[Incom Corporation/Legends|Incom Corporation]] assembly facility, Eirriss was one several pilots called to a briefing in which [[General/Legends|General]] [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]] outlined a plan to capture the fighters by using four [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]], recently brought to the Alliance by the defecting crew of the [[Imperial II-class frigate|''Imperial II''-class frigate]] [[Rand Ecliptic/Legends|''Rand Ecliptic'']], to draw the defending TIE Fighters away from the nearby [[Imperial Security Bureau/Legends|Imperial Security Bureau]] base. With the facility temporarily undefended, the ''Rand Ecliptic'' would arrive with a [[GR-75 medium transport/Legends|GR-75 medium transport]] to retrieve the X-wings. The mission was a success and the Rebels retrieved several X-wings, although [[Peate Kurin]] was killed and [[Derek Klivian/Legends|Derek Klivian]] lost a leg during the diversionary raid. Soon after the mission, Eirriss was able to practice in the captured X-wings along with [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], [[Biggs Darklighter/Legends|Biggs Darklighter]] and [[Jek Tono Porkins/Legends|Jek Tono Porkins]], before the four went to visit Klivian as he recovered in the infirmary. ===Mission to Commenor=== Although the X-wings had relieved the Alliance's immediate shortage of starfighters, they were still critically short of the [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droids]] required to help the pilots operate the [[Starship/Legends|ships]]. The Empire, anticipating their need, had begun impounding the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] to keep them out of Rebel hands, but the Alliance had located a source when [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Nera Dantels]] of the civilian [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] ''[[Starduster]]'' arranged to rendezvous with Alliance forces on the Imperial stronghold of [[Commenor/Legends|Commenor]] to deliver a shipment of [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2]] and [[R5-series astromech droid|R5]] units. Dodonna decided to keep personnel and ships to a minimum by launching an [[Mission to Commenor (0 BBY)|undercover operation]] to retrieve the droids. Eirriss was selected for the mission, along with Antilles, Darklighter and Porkins, and was briefed by the general before departing for Commenor. [[File:EirrissFighting.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Eirriss in a brawl on Commenor]] The four Rebels headed for Commenor with Eirriss flying under the [[Callsign/Legends|call sign]] "[[Red Four/Legends|Red Four]]." After landing their X-wings at the [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] on Commenor, the Rebels rented a [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]] and proceeded to a local [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]], where they had been told to play a game of [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]] while waiting for Dantels. While they played, the Rebels heard the latest news of the [[Dissolution of the Imperial Senate/Legends|dissolution of the Imperial Senate]] on the [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]], learning of the apparent disappearance of [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] of the planet{{DB|location|alderaan|Alderaan}} [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]. Eirriss dropped out of the game when the bet got too high, leaving Porkins to go on and win. Eirriss suggested a game of [[null-gravity caroms]] next, but before they could start a new game, Antilles received a signal from Dantels directing them to a nearby [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]], and the group departed. Outside the cantina, the Rebels were met by a local gang who attempted to elicit a protection fee by claiming to have chased thieves away from their landspeeder. When one of them insulted Porkins, the Rebel responded with a knee to the youth's midsection and a brawl broke out between the two groups. The fight came to an abrupt end when the Rebels drew their [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] and made a quick exit before Imperial authorities arrived. Eirriss and the group proceeded to [[Hangar Bay 31]] where they were due to rendezvous with Dantels. After confirming their identity, Dantels took the Rebels to the ''Starduster'', where Eirriss quickly checked to see if the droids appeared to be in working order. Satisfied, the Rebels arranged to meet up with Dantels in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]], after retrieving their X-wings, and lead the ''Starduster'' back to Yavin 4. However, they were unaware that one of the street gang had followed them to the rendezvous and alerted a [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] to the meeting. The stormtrooper called the Imperial base on the nearby [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Folor]] to warn them of the fleeing Rebels and, by the time the Rebels reached orbit, the Imperials had already dispatched TIE/LN starfighters to intercept them over Commenor. ===Death=== {{Quote|Doc's gone, and there's nothing we can do about that…but we can send the Death Star to hell in her name!|Wedge Antilles|}} [[File:Cesi_Eirriss_flight_helmet.jpg|thumb|left|240px|"Doc" flew her X-wing as part of Rogue Squadron over Commenor.]] Seeing the incoming fighters, Antilles instructed Eirris and Porkins to provide cover for the ''Starduster'' while he and Darklighter moved to [[Starfighter combat/Legends|engage]] the Imperials. Eirriss and Porkins were soon engaged with TIEs that had broken past Antilles and Darklighter but were able to eliminate the TIEs, with each destroying their [[Wingmate/Legends|wingmate's]] pursuers. However, while the Rebels were occupied, more TIEs attacked the ''Starduster''. Dantels used her ship's [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] to destroy the TIEs, but Eirriss's X-wing was struck by the [[Solar collector/Legends|solar panel]] from one of the Imperial fighters, damaging her [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generator]] and [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]]. Since Eirriss was unable to handle the necessary repairs on her own, Antilles ordered her to pressurize her [[Rebel flight suit|flight suit]] and eject to be picked up by the ''Starduster''. With a new wave of TIEs incoming, Eirriss instead told the others to make the jump to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] and set a collision course for the approaching fighters. Eirriss was killed in a head-on collision that destroyed her X-wing and a number of TIEs, but her actions provided the time needed for her comrades to flee the [[Commenor system/Legends|Commenor system]]. When the Empire's [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] later moved against the Rebel base on Yavin 4, the survivors of the Commenor mission vowed to destroy it in Eirriss's name. Her place in the squadron for the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] was filled by [[John D. Branon/Legends|John D. Branon]]. Though Eirriss died before Antilles and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] formed [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] following the Battle of Yavin, she was included in a [[Hologram/Legends|holographic]] memorial to the [[Squadron/Legends|squadron's]] dead[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 35|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 35]] as late as [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]].''[[Star Wars Omnibus: X-Wing Rogue Squadron Volume 3]]'' places the events of ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron: Mandatory Retirement]]'' approximately one year after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|That lady had guts to spare.|Biggs Darklighter on Cesi Eirriss|}} [[File:EirrissDeath.jpg|thumb|right|360px|Eirriss gave her life to counter the TIE attack on her squadron.]] Despite having a doctorate in political science, Cesi Eirriss chose to join the Rebel Alliance in their struggle against the Galactic Empire. She was a capable pilot, who developed a good relationship with the other members of Red Squadron and was also capable of handling herself in a fight, helping her wingmates to defeat the street thugs on Commenor. Eirriss believed that making a sacrifice for the greater good was worthwhile. After Dodonna outlined his plan to capture X-wings from the Incom facility, Darklighter expressed his concerns over the need to sacrifice the ''Rand Ecliptic''{{'s}} TIE Fighters, but Eirriss stated that it was a small price to pay and spoke of her respect for Dodonna's tactical ability. When her starfighter suffered damage during the Commenor mission, Eirriss was ultimately willing to sacrifice her life in order to give her comrades time to escape. Eiriss was a [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] with [[Color/Legends|olive-green]] skin who had maroon or pink lips and green [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] defined by dark shades around the rim, sometimes with additional turquoise smudged above the eye. She curled one of her [[Lekku/Legends|lekku]] around her chest. ==Equipment== As an X-wing pilot, Eirriss wore an orange [[Rebel flight suit]] with a brown [[Holster/Legends|holster]] by her right waist for a [[DL-44 heavy blaster pistol/Legends|DL-44 heavy blaster pistol]]. She donned black [[Glove/Legends|gloves]] and [[Boot/Legends|boots]], with a loop of ammunition below her right knee. White headgear wrapped around the top of her lekku and enclosed the sides of her head, and it extended to enclose her neck. Eirriss' [[Flight helmet/Legends|flight helmet]] accomodated the upper portions of her lekku and was bulbous and white, with an amber visor that extended down to her nose, a white chinstrap, yellow stripes slanting to her left over a white panel at the front of the helmet, and orange dots over the rims around the sides of her head. On Commenor, Eirriss fashioned a pair of boots, [[Belt/Legends|belt]], and [[Jacket/Legends|jacket]] in black over a set of dark olive-gray [[Clothing/Legends|tunic]] and trousers with a black line running down its length. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:CesiActionFigure.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Cesi Eirriss's action figure]] Cesi Eirriss was created by [[Michael A. Stackpole]] for the one-shot [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] ''[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron ½]]'', published on [[February 1]], [[1997]].{{DarkHorse|url=Products/96-789/Star-Wars-X-Wing-Rogue-Squadron-1-2-Special-Wizard-Magazine-comic|text=Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron #1/2 (Special Wizard Magazine comic)}} She later made a brief appearance as a hologram in [[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 35|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 35]] and appeared in the [[Empire 12|third issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars: Empire: Darklighter]]'' story arc by [[Paul Chadwick]], which was set around the events of ''X-wing Rogue Squadron ½'' and included a brief recap of that story. She also received entries in [[Jason Fry]]'s [[Star Wars Insider 59|''Star Wars Insider'' 59]] article ''[[Who's Who in Rogue Squadron]]'' and ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.{{InsiderCite|59|Who's Who in Rogue Squadron}}{{CSWECite|I|227|Eirriss, Cesi "Doc"}} In [[2008]], an Eirriss action figure was included in an "Evolutions" [[W:c:swmerchandise:Rebel Pilot Legacy Series II (87653)|three-pack]] of Rebel pilots as part of [[Hasbro]]'s ''[[Star Wars: The Legacy Collection]]''.{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: The Legacy Collection|text=''Star Wars Evolutions''|pack=Rebel Pilot Legacy (Series 2)|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/tlcEVORebelPilots2.asp}} Whereas ''Rogue Squadron'' ½ showed Eirriss with [[Ear/Legends|ears]] and pale white skin, she had olive-green skin with the sides of her head covered in ''Empire'' 12, ''Rogue Squadron 35'', and her Hasbro action figure and its corresponding packaging illustration. This article assumes that the first comic issue is in error. ==Appearances== *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron ½|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' ½]] {{1st}} *[[Empire 12|''Empire'' 12]] *[[Empire 15|''Empire'' 15]] {{Flash}} *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 35|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 35]] {{Hologram}} ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Cesi Eirriss}} *{{InsiderCite|59|Who's Who in Rogue Squadron}} *''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' {{Imo}} *{{FactFile|24|DAR 1-4|Biggs Darklighter}} *''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]'' *{{FactFile|88|COM 1-2|Commenor}} {{Po}} *{{InsiderCite|76|Comics Link}} {{Po}} *{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: The Legacy Collection|text=''Star Wars Evolutions''|pack=Rebel Pilot Legacy (Series 2)|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/tlcEVORebelPilots2.asp}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' *''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Action Figure Collection]]'' *{{FactFile2014|40|DAR 1-4|Biggs Darklighter}} *{{InsiderCite|154|Authors of the Expanded Universe|Authors of the Expanded Universe: The Legend of Dark Horse}} {{Po}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Cesi Eirriss |es=Cesi Eirriss |nl=Cesi Eirriss |pl=Cesi Eirriss |uk=Цесі Ейррісс }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Eirriss, Cesi}} [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Individuals with she/her pronouns]] [[Category:Red Squadron personnel]] [[Category:Twi'leks]]
